disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Sudden onset (about two hours ago) of severe, unrelenting pain in the left calf and foot, not relieved by rest or usual analgesia
trigger_question_examples: When did the pain start and how quickly did it come on?
What does the pain feel like and does anything ease it?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It started very suddenly about two hours ago and it's been unbearable the whole time — nothing has helped, not rest and not his usual pain medicine.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The left foot and lower leg are pale/mottled and icy cold to touch with a clear line of change at about mid-calf
trigger_question_examples: What does the leg look like — the colour and temperature?
Is one leg colder or a different colour than the other?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: The foot and lower leg have gone really pale and blotchy and they feel icy cold — there's a clear line about halfway up the calf where it changes.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Sensory loss beginning as toe pins-and-needles and progressing rapidly to complete numbness of the foot
trigger_question_examples: Can he feel the foot normally?
Is there any numbness or pins and needles?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It started as pins and needles in the toes and now he can't feel the foot at all.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Motor deficit — the patient cannot wiggle the toes or lift the foot (new weakness/foot drop)
trigger_question_examples: Can he move the foot or wiggle the toes?
Is there any new weakness in the foot?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No — he can't move his toes or lift his foot at all now, which is new.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Staff could not hear a pulse in the foot with a hand-held Doppler
trigger_question_examples: Have the staff been able to feel or hear a pulse in the foot?
Does the facility have a Doppler and what did it show?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: The nurses tried a hand-held Doppler on the foot and couldn't pick up any pulse.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Background history of intermittent claudication and known peripheral arterial disease
trigger_question_examples: Does he have any history of circulation problems in the legs?
Has he had leg pain on walking before?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes — the chart says he has poor circulation in the legs and gets cramping leg pain when he walks.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No redness, warmth, swelling or fever in the leg
trigger_question_examples: Is the leg red, hot, swollen, or is he feverish?
Any signs of infection — redness spreading, warmth, or a temperature?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No — it's not red or hot or swollen, and he doesn't have a fever. If anything it's pale and cold.
